segments.text: These are all of the planets we've ever
found outside our solar system. But what
is that? If you look at it from Earth,
it looks like that. Back in 2009, most
of the very few exoplanets that we'd
ever found were big, gassy ones like
Jupiter. But were there more like Earth?
NASA launched a telescope to find out.
It was more sensitive than any planet
hunter ever before. And inside it looked
like this. You recognize that it snapped
photos in a grid like this in one spot
of sky for 4 years. This isn't some big
line of planets. It's just where we
chose to look and we're still looking.
If you need more optimistic stories,
